title: Positive aspiration! Do I need to throw away the syringe？
filename: Positive aspiration! Do I need to throw away the syringe？.en.vtt
excerpt: So if you get a positive aspiration do you need to throw away your dermatologist [Music] so um very common question and really if you think about the situation we're injecting the patient it's their blood you've sucked a little bit of blood back in the syringe and you now have bl…

segments.text: so if you get a positive aspiration do you need to throw away your dermatologist [Music] so um very common question and really if you think about the situation we're injecting the patient it's their blood you've sucked a little bit of blood back in the syringe and you now have blood usually at the top of your syringe now in these cases you can basically take the needle off and squirt that into your sink so that you don't lose all the filler but you get rid of all the blood and then put a fresh needle on and then continue injecting and this will save you wasting you know often 120 pounds worth of product for you and so very expensive mistake if you have to throw it away now some aspirations unfortunately and i've had this a couple of times with a big aspirate that's positive it actually tracks down the side of the syringe and actually mixes with all the products so there's no product that you can't simply just inject the blood out and continue so there's blood mixed in with the filler i would not recommend injecting that because blood is inflammatory it will cause pain it will cause bruising you're almost injecting bruises and in those cases you need to throw away the product and start again but for
most cases if you can get rid of the blood at the end of the needle and put a fresh needle on you can continue using that cylinder of filler hope that helps you

keyPoints.text: So if you get a positive aspiration do you need to throw away your dermatologist [Music] so um very common question and really if you think about the situation we're injecting the patient it's their blood you've sucked a…

transcript: so if you get a positive aspiration do you need to throw away your dermatologist [Music] so um very common question and really if you think about the situation we're injecting the patient it's their blood you've sucked a little bit of blood back in the syringe and you now have blood usually at the top of your syringe now in these cases you can basically take the needle off and squirt that into your sink so that you don't lose all the filler but you get rid of all the blood and then put a fresh needle on and then continue injecting and this will save you wasting you know often 120 pounds worth of product for you and so very expensive mistake if you have to throw it away now some aspirations unfortunately and i've had this a couple of times with a big aspirate that's positive it actually tracks down the side of the syringe and actually mixes with all the products so there's no product that you can't simply just inject the blood out and continue so there's blood mixed in with the filler i would not recommend injecting that because blood is inflammatory it will cause pain it will cause bruising you're almost injecting bruises and in those cases you need to throw away the product and start again but for most cases if you can get rid of the blood at the end of the needle and put a fresh needle on you can continue using that cylinder of filler hope that helps you
